text:
### 3.8 Counterfactual diff — `tenex-edge probe diff <capsule> --mutate <step>.<field>=<value>`

*"What if this input had been different?"* — the interaction that is uniquely cheap because plans are data, and the one no log-based system can offer. Run two `TrellisHarness` replays of the same capsule: verbatim, and with one drive-arg mutated (a channel added to a `CoverageSnapshot`, `working=true` flipped, a presence row removed, the cursor zeroed). Diff the two scenarios step-by-step: commands emitted/suppressed, frames Baseline/Delta/Unchanged, receipt deltas — rendered as a per-step `±` table, `--json` for agents. Uses `Scenario::assert_step_resource_commands`-style comparison, inverted to *report* rather than assert. This is both a debugging tool ("would the leak have happened if the session had left cleanly?") and a value demonstration ("here is the exact minimal decision-diff for a one-fact change — try producing that from your logs").
